{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks about the movement of the permanent second molar when the permanent first molar is lost. In dental anatomy, when a tooth is lost, the adjacent teeth tend to move towards the empty space. The first molar is located mesially to the second molar. Therefore, when the first molar is lost, the second molar would move towards the mesial direction to fill the gap.", "answer_choice": "C"}
